original_abstract: Universal human values function as the primary foundation for forging national character and fostering a just, peaceful, and prosperous society. Within the complex landscape of globalization and modernization, the early cultivation of these values has become increasingly vital. As a core component of the Indonesian school curriculum, Pancasila Education holds a strategic position in instilling these principles. This study explores the significant potential of integrating Hindu philosophy—which possesses a profound reservoir of universal ethical concepts—into the Pancasila Education framework. By examining key philosophical tenets such as *Ahimsa* (non-violence), *Tat Tvam Asi* (human unity), and *Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam* (universal brotherhood), this research highlights how these spiritual insights can enrich the moral development of the younger generation. The findings suggest that such an integration assists students in evolving into ethical individuals with noble character and a deep-seated respect for humanity. Despite challenges regarding teacher preparedness and the availability of relevant instructional materials, the synergy between Hindu philosophical values and Pancasila Education offers a robust pathway for strengthening the nation's future social fabric and democratic integrity.
humanized_abstract: It is acknowledged unanimously that universal human values provide a foundation for the shaping of a nation and for achieving a nation that is just, peaceful and prosperous. With the process of globalization and modernization taking place today, it is very important for children to learn universal human values early in life. One of the aims of education in Indonesia is the implementation of Pancasila Education in schools, with the purpose of forming individuals that embrace universal human values. A variety of studies have confirmed that universal human values are widely included in Hindu Philosophy. Therefore, this study wishes to explore the possibilities of applying universal human values, based on the elements of Hindu Philosophy, namely with Ahimsa (non-violence), Tat Tvam Asi (Man is One), and Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam (we are one global human family) in order to enrich values-based learning through Pancasila Education in Schools. Research data reveal that Indonesian students have the potential to evolve into citizens with moral values, a personality that upholds justice and an awareness of global citizenship after adopting the universal human values from Hindu Philosophy within the implementation of Pancasila Education in Schools. It has been found that there are several challenges which need to be reviewed by academics, namely teachers’ capacities who will implement the policy and the provision of learning materials, hence there will be many more opportunities that enhance the implementation of the nation’s Philosophy for the building of children who form the nation’s future as it strives to achieve social and political development with high ethical values for a civil society and strong democratic systems of governance.
